hard.next_step: 5.26.26 KC: Emailed Shreya to make sure we're tracking. Following schedule outline below. Already gave demo so plan to learn what their scoring criteria is.
hard.ais_why:
• Cadence has not had any external Avoma-recorded discussion on this iRisk opportunity yet.
• Activity is a vendor-led RFP (received 4/28, questions due 5/1) with no AI-specific framing - the scope is iRisk, not Merlin or ANA.
• Opportunity is in Formal Evaluation but on Cadence's clock, so AI excitement cannot yet be measured from prospect voice.

north_star_verdict.headline: A stalled $65k single-module iRisk evaluation: the buyer has gone quiet for ~47 days since the 20 May POC wrap-up, no economic buyer or champion is engaged, the close date already slipped from 30 Jun to 31 Jul, and the deal has been reassigned to Karson Keogh with no fresh buyer touch. On current pace it will not close on 31 Jul.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 28 Apr, 69 days old; 25 days to close (31 Jul). Stuck in Formal Evaluation since 13 May, zero stage progression. Close date pushed 30 Jun to 31 Jul on 16 Jun (a slip). Owner reassigned twice (Kevin Cipollaro to Mario Castro 29 May, to Karson Keogh 14 Jun). Last buyer-facing event 20 May POC wrap-up (~47 days ago); rep emailed Shreya 26 May per Next_Step__c with no reply (~41 days). No EB engaged, no champion, no commercial or contracting step in motion. Required pace to close in 25 days (EB engagement, architecture review, pricing, contracting) is not remotely matched by ~47 days of buyer silence.
evidence: Last buyer-facing event was the 20 May 2026 iRisk POC wrap-up, ~47 days ago (prior sweep living memory)
Rep emailed Shreya 26 May per Next_Step__c; no buyer reply in ~41 days
Close date pushed 30 Jun to 31 Jul on 16 Jun by Karson Keogh (field history)
Opportunity reassigned twice between 29 May and 14 Jun (Kevin Cipollaro to Mario Castro to Karson Keogh), signalling low priority
No OpportunityContactRole on the deal: economic buyer and decision maker unmapped, single-threaded through Shreya
Stuck in Formal Evaluation since 13 May with no pricing, commercial, or contracting step in motion

metrics.narrative: Cadence has articulated qualitative pain (manual contract-repository search, regulatory compliance/risk visibility, invoice-matching inefficiency) but has never shared a quantified impact, savings target, or ROI figure. The 20 May POC wrap-up recorded consensus that the iRisk POC 'met needs', yet no cycle-time, headcount, or dollar figure was stated by the buyer, and AIS_Why__c confirms this is a vendor-led RFP with no AI/value framing. Without a quantified business case the budget threshold and forecast defensibility stay weak; quantifying it with Cadence's finance lead is a ranked move.
sources: Prior sweep Avoma POC wrap-up 20 May 2026

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is mapped: Salesforce carries no OpportunityContactRole at all for this deal, no budget-authority conversation is on record, and the only named live contact is Shreya (Next_Step__c 26 May 2026), a coordinator with no stated budget power. Prior living memory notes ~21 touchpoints with no exec-level Cadence participant and a 'consensus-driven decision' at the 20 May POC wrap-up, but never named who owns the budget or final sign-off. Twenty-five days from a 31 Jul close, surfacing and engaging the budget owner is the single highest-priority move; without it the deal cannot reach commercial negotiation.
sources: Prior sweep Avoma POC wrap-up 20 May 2026

decision_criteria.narrative: Cadence is scoring vendors against structured RFP criteria but has not shared the criteria, rankings, or finalist shortlist with the relevant stakeholder_Step__c 26 May states the rep's 'plan to learn what their scoring criteria is', confirming the framework exists but remains opaque to us, and the 20 May POC wrap-up recorded only that iRisk 'met needs'. Cadence committed on 20 May to share the criteria; 47 days later it is undelivered, so Zycus cannot pressure-test where it ranks or tailor the close.
sources: Prior sweep Avoma POC wrap-up 20 May 2026

decision_process.narrative: Cadence is running a consensus-driven evaluation with a mandatory enterprise architecture-review gate before final approval, per the 20 May POC wrap-up, but the review timeline, owner, pass/fail criteria, and final sign-off authority remain unknown. The process has been on Cadence's own clock since qualification on 28 Apr with no stage progression since 13 May. On the Zycus side the deal has itself churned owners three times (Kevin Cipollaro to the relevant stakeholder to Karson Keogh), which has cost continuity; getting the review structure and timeline in writing is needed to plan around the gate.
sources: Prior sweep Avoma POC wrap-up 20 May 2026

paper_process.narrative: No contracting discussion has occurred: MSA/SOW, legal timeline, InfoSec, and procurement channel are all undefined, and this single-module iRisk + iSaaS scope would likely close on an Order Form plus SOW rather than a full-suite MSA. Prior living memory flagged migration/integration scoping (iManage extraction methodology, Coupa middleware) as open on the 21 Apr and 28 Apr calls, still unresolved. With the architecture-review gate not yet timelined and no commercial step opened, the 31 Jul close date is unreachable on paper mechanics alone.
sources: Prior sweep Avoma calls 21 Apr and 28 Apr 2026
Zycus contracting model (single-module = Order Form + SOW)

identify_pain.narrative: Cadence articulated three operational pains: manual, time-consuming search across its contract repository; regulatory compliance complexity requiring centralized risk visibility; and invoice-processing inefficiency with vendor/PO matching gaps. The 20 May POC wrap-up confirmed the iRisk POC 'met needs' for contract-risk visibility. The pain is real and acknowledged across legal, finance, and IT, but no single pain owner has stepped forward as champion or budget owner, and the impact is not yet quantified.
sources: Prior sweep Avoma POC wrap-up 20 May 2026

champion.narrative: No champion exists. Shreya is the sole live point of contact (the rep emailed her 26 May per Next_Step__c) but has not replied in ~41 days and has shown no advocacy, no economic-buyer access, and no ability to drive internal momentum. The deal is single-threaded through a passive coordinator, and Salesforce holds zero contact roles to multi-thread from. Building a real champion with budget-owner access ranks second only to surfacing the economic buyer itself.

competition.narrative: Zero competitive intelligence. Cadence is running an RFP with multiple vendors (per the 20 May scoring-criteria discussion) but has not disclosed the shortlist, rankings, or alternatives, and no competitor name appears in any Salesforce field, task, Next_Step, or (this sweep) any call. This is a complete blind spot: Zycus cannot differentiate or pressure-test win probability without knowing the field. The dominant threat may in fact be no-decision on Cadence's own clock rather than a named rival. Surfacing the shortlist and Zycus's rank is a top move for the next 14 to 30 days.
sources: Prior sweep Avoma POC wrap-up 20 May 2026

deal_movement.summary: The pulse is negative and cooling: after the 20 May iRisk POC wrap-up the buyer went quiet, the rep's 26 May email to Shreya drew no reply, the close date slipped 30 Jun to 31 Jul on 16 Jun, and the deal changed hands twice before landing with Karson Keogh on 14 Jun. No forward buyer engagement has followed.

items.change: Stage moved Qualified to Formal Evaluation (no progression since)
Rep emailed Shreya to confirm tracking; no buyer reply since
Opportunity reassigned Kevin Cipollaro to Mario Castro
Opportunity reassigned Mario Castro to Karson Keogh
Close date pushed 30 Jun to 31 Jul by Karson Keogh

competitive_position.summary: Competitive landscape is unknown. Cadence is running an RFP with an undisclosed vendor shortlist and has shared neither its scoring criteria nor rankings; no rival is named in any field, task, next-step, or call. The single strongest current threat is most plausibly no-decision/inertia on Cadence's own evaluation clock rather than any named vendor, given a 47-day silence and a slipped date. Surfacing the shortlist is the priority before any competitor can be ranked.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Product fit for contract-risk visibility is validated, but process/architecture fit and commercial fit are unproven and undelivered by the buyer.

best_practice_check.summary: Three win-strategy levers matter most now: rebuild threads to find power, close the competitive blind spot, and treat the slipped date as real.
flags: Single-threaded through a non-responsive coordinator (Shreya); multi-thread into legal, finance, and IT to find a budget owner and a champion.
Competitive blind spot: an active RFP with an undisclosed shortlist; surface the rivals and Zycus's rank before any pricing move.
Deal reassigned twice and close date pushed to 31 Jul; treat the date as at-risk and co-author a fresh decision timeline with Cadence.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 5.0 (Moderate) per AIS_Why__c: no external Avoma discussion on this iRisk opportunity, a vendor-led RFP with no AI-specific framing, scope is iRisk not Merlin. No call evidence this sweep to over- or under-rate it.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A stalled $65k single-module iRisk evaluation at Cadence: the iRisk POC met the team's needs on 20 May, but the buyer has gone quiet for ~47 days, the deal has changed hands three times since late May, no economic buyer is engaged, and the close date already slipped from 30 Jun to 31 Jul.

win_position.text: Zycus stands on a technically validated footing: the iRisk POC was judged to meet Cadence's contract-risk needs at the 20 May wrap-up, and the underlying pain (manual contract search, compliance visibility, invoice matching) is real and acknowledged.

No economic buyer has ever engaged and there is no champion: Shreya, the only live contact (Next_Step__c 26 May), coordinates but does not advocate or hold budget, so nothing is driving an internal buy.
Cadence is running an RFP with an undisclosed shortlist and has not shared its scoring criteria or Zycus's rank, so the fit win is unverified against rivals we cannot see.

deal_momentum.text: The buyer has not engaged in ~47 days since the 20 May POC wrap-up, and the rep's 26 May email to Shreya drew no reply.
Momentum is deteriorating: the close date slipped 30 Jun to 31 Jul on 16 Jun and the deal was reassigned twice (Kevin Cipollaro to Mario Castro to Karson Keogh) between 29 May and 14 Jun.
The deal has not moved out of Formal Evaluation since 13 May, with no pricing, commercial, or contracting step in motion 25 days from close.

customer_commitment.text: Cadence committed on 20 May to share its RFP scoring criteria and the architecture-review timeline; neither has arrived 47 days later.
Engagement is single-threaded through Shreya with no customer-named dates, no signed next meeting, and no buyer-requested follow-up.

deal_risk.text: No economic buyer is mapped and no champion exists 25 days from a close date that already slipped once, so the deal cannot reach negotiation in time without exec sponsorship.
Competitive position is a blind spot: an active RFP with an undisclosed shortlist leaves open a surprise loss to an unknown finalist.
The primary threat may be no-decision/inertia: Cadence keeps evaluating on its own clock and could simply not select a vendor before 31 Jul.

seniority.evidence: No exec-level Cadence participant on any call; economic buyer unmapped, zero contact roles.

engagement.evidence: Buyer silent ~47 days; unanswered 26 May email to Shreya.

timeline.evidence: Close date pushed 30 Jun to 31 Jul on 16 Jun; no commercial motion 25 days out.

champion.evidence: No champion; single-threaded through a non-responsive coordinator.

scope_change.detail: Single-module iRisk (plus iSaaS platform) scope unchanged at $65k; no narrowing or expansion evidenced this sweep.

critical_signals.text: The last real engagement, the 20 May iRisk POC wrap-up, ended with the team agreeing the solution met their contract-risk needs, but nothing has advanced in the 47 days since.
Cadence is running a multi-vendor RFP but has never disclosed its shortlist or scoring, leaving no visibility into who Zycus is up against or where it ranks.
No budget owner has engaged and no pricing or commercial conversation has opened on this $65k single-module iRisk deal, 25 days from the close date.

customer_preference.evidence: iRisk POC 'met needs' on 20 May, but no Zycus-specific lean, no competitor comparison, and buyer silent ~47 days; next step is only to 'learn what their scoring criteria is'.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain (manual contract search, compliance visibility, invoice-matching gaps) articulated; no cost, headcount, cycle-time, or ROI figure quantified by the buyer.

commentary.win_position: Win 15 is carried by business case strength +0.30 (weight 10). Offsetting it: momentum 44 vs stage-expected 52.
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 44 (slipping backward): Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (15), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 44, and dragged by risk 14. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 1 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
